reviews.review:
A very fun, competitive game.

If you like Rocket League, you'll probably like this.

Would love to see the community grow.

It's pretty cool and I suspect I could get a lot better. Like Rocket League it feels like a game that is simple in principle where the potential skill you can have has almost no limit. Unfortunately Steam has botched the free weekend download and I cannot get it on my laptop since it's "already in my Steam library". Even though the title is not visible in my library. Whoops.

Great combination of Atari's PONG and Nintendo's Mario Tennis. It is good to play online with some sort of ranked matches.
I have played around 100 matches and, in some of them (less than 5), a bug just happened during the start of the match. When someone scores the first point it looks like a paused match for a while, with everything else working fine (like menu and chat). After some seconds / minutes, it just continue like nothing happened.  This bug did not affected negatively me during any match.
2-0'd Brandon then refund
Honestly should be a free game. There is not enough content for this to be worth $15, plus you have to purchase things in game on top of that.

Pretty fun game, I find it quite expensive for what it is. 
Buy on sale.

amazing game!
Good job!
lots of fun, lag is a serious issue in some matches but finding a game takes less than 5 seconds at any hour which is nice.

If you only play one Robot Frisbee Tennis game this year, make it Disc Jam.   It's a very polished and fun game.  As a fan of Rocket League I was naturally drawn to try this.  It's 1v1 and 2v2 matches.  You can team up or go it alone.  I had fun with regular matches but even more fun playing 1v1 with a friend.  It's cross platform. Also, you can invite and join friends even if you are not logged in to 'Steam Friends and Chat'   There are other game modes too.   

The tutorial could be improved.  Some tutorials tell you "Too early" or "Too Late" but for some, it says "Perfect" even if you are doing it wrong.  Tell us what we are doing wrong!!  The only other concern is the amount of people online has been low historically but it's recently spiked due to a promotional free weekend, something I suggest be done periodically.
its fun, and cheap when on sale

Really fun game to just jump in and spend some time , playied it while it was free and was pretty fun , would buy it on a sale but cant justify the full price for a game that only has 3 modes and ingame transactions for customizations .
great fun
Addicting fun

cross platform ($22 on PSN store) and you will still struggle to find a match...shame really as it looks great is loads of fun to play...that said I am never going to sink money into what is essentially a 2018 version of PONG   lolz
Kinda like baby Rocket League. Very fun.

Fun to play, but it should definitely be free to play. Disc Jam is a nice competitive weird-version-of-tennis-but-isn't-tennis game, which makes it fun to play with a buddy in online 2 v 2's. The gameplay struggle this game has is desync and lag. Sometimes it clearly looks like a player is not in position to catch something, but somehow manages to(which throws off your reactions completely).

I would strongly suggest the creators of this game to make this f2p. It took a while to be put into matches during a free to play weekend, I'm estimating that queue times will take more than 5 minutes when it isn't. The cool part about this game is that it has crossplay to PS4, but I imagine it doesn't bring that many players either.

Played this game on a free weekend. It has the semblance of a good game but is buried under some questionable decisions.

Disc Jam is a cross between Windjammers and Lethal League with multiple tech and throw options. However, the problem is the window to do anything is ridiculously strict. I assume if I had more time with it, I could get the timing down but the problem there also is one of the buttons is both a throw and deflect move. What that means is when you think you've caught the disc, instead what you do is deflect it and it goes haywire and really messes you up instead of you just throwing it because you're trying to time a perfect throw with that button. Even if you remap that button, those commands will always go together. I would have preferred the deflect being on one of the triggers as both are a curve move that are influenced by the direction you're pressing and not what trigger you're holding down. Many of my opponents fell victim to this also and many points were scored by myself or the opponent attempting to do a perfect throw and instead, the shield making the disc flop to the ground and the other scoring because of that. Other times, there seemed to be lag where catching the disc would be extremely lenient in some cases.

There seems to be no quick play / casual mode. You are forced to play only ranked with other random players online. This might not matter to some but other players don't care about a medal on their profile and some could even be too coaxed up in what their rank is as opposed to just having fun. There are lobbies also but invite only.

The biggest problem I have is that this game is paid but also has loot boxes with paid currency. Sure, you can bring up the usual excuses of "it's just cosmetic", "it's optional" and "they need to make money somehow" but those arguments have been refuted numerous times (cosmetics are an incentive therefore you want to stand out and have good gear; it's either that or grind for ages; DLC packs are a much better option as you know what you're getting as opposed to paying money for currency and MAYBE getting what you want). There is the option of just buying stuff outright but two of the characters are 25k currency each. That's either hours and hours of grinding or £10. It's just iffy and I wish they monetized this in basically any other way.

Some positives though is that it does have an arcade mode (albeit not much added to it), the customisation is neat, having options for different discs and whatnot, the music is good and it has crossplay with the Switch and PS4 so if you know someone that might be interested in this but can't get it on PC.

I feel this game might be more fun with a friend for doubles but otherwise, nah. Not for me, unfortunately.

it's kinda like a fusion of dodgeball and mario tennis, with rocket league's interface and ui.  

fun at least for a few hours or to play with friends, but get it while it's on sale.
Greatest game I've ever played
Good fun game to play with a friend. Some lag that can be frustrating and can ruin good games. Worth a try on a discount, but not worth full price imo.

A pretty [b]fast-paced[/b] game which is quite fun to play with friends. It's essentially like [b]Windjammers[/b] (Neo Geo), but with modern graphics, multiplayer features etc.

If you've enjoyed it, you [i]might[/i] enjoy this one too. It can be seen as a [b]casual[/b] game, but it's actually quite [b]competitive[/b]. I wouldn't recommend it if you tend to rage when losing.

I had a few issues with matchmaking, it took a few minutes to find a game and connect to the server, but nothing major I'd say.

Great game, althoguh i bought it ages ago, the player base in asia region is less so it took forever to find a game, recently it has been free and i have been finding games a lot and its fun and has a good learning curve.

Discord community is great and very helpful, I would suggest to Try to learn the game and how the playstyle is before juding it within 30 minutes.

I have no idea why people are complaining about the mictrotransactions - they're purely cosmetic as far as I can tell. "It takes hundreds of hours to unlock basic stuff hurr durr" these reviewers are braindead and of course they all have comments disabled so you can't point out their misgivings.

The gameplay itself is solid but I think it'll remain addicting for 10h at best. Still worth a buy at 6e.. but for the full price? Nah.

This Is A very fun game with addictive qualities that put it up there with the likes of rocket league, what lets it down though is greed, free to play monatisation in a game you have to pay for is never a good thing as a lot of the AAA developers/publishers are finding out at the moment.
That said and as fun as i find this game to play I will not be buying it and cannot recommend it to anyone else

one final note, not sure whether to click the i recieved this product for free check as i have not payed for the game and have only played the free weekend, so full disclosure there
best $6 i ever spent

I got a lot more entertainment out of this game than I thought I would during the free weekend. Simply from teaming up with a friend and playing against others online. Yes it's get repetitive, but repetition isn't bad when the thing that works, works well. However, this is definitely a game that is better played with someone else. I'm heading back up to college soon, and I think this would be solid pick for couch party gaming.

I'll see if my friends have any interest in it, and I'll pick it up if they do. I think $15 is an okay price for this game, however the ingame shops are confusing to say the least. Nonetheless, it's got my recommendation.

Space Jam (1996) was a cultural phenoman of the pre-2000s. Never has human emotion been expressed so beautifully in such a poetic way. The fight between the Looney Toons and the bad guy basketball team had me on the edge of seat. My only complaint is that Lola Bunny didn't have enought screen time. I am a lead researcher in the War Against the Furries and I needed more footage of Lola for my latest scientific research. Other than that, this was a great movie. 9/5 Mastahpiece.

Fast paced game. Takes a while to master everything. Only played for a little. Can't really recommend one way or the other so I just gave thumbs up.

The idea and mechanics of this game are very interesting essentially it's a competitive frisbee game where you use lobs, bounces, quick shots and curve throws to score points by either hitting the back wall or hitting the ground in your opponents field. you can also use super shots that move in unpredictable ways by catching a disc in midair and returning it instantly or catching another supershot and returning it instantly and a perfect shot which moves faster by throwing a disc the moment you get it, you also have a shield which you can use to block shots upwards either to your field or the oponents depending on which direction you are moving allowing you to counter poorly made shots or set up supers for yourself, however the speed at which the discs move (especially super and perfect shots) feels way too high compared to your characters movement speed to make matters worse you also only have about a second-ish to throw your disc if you don't you'll just do a basic throw which clashes with the tacticle nature of your character abilities. to make matters worse curve shots require you to move the left stick in a specific motion like in fighting games which makes it even harder to pull off.

Over all I can see why people like this but it's just way too clunky and sluggish for me to recommend.
gamesense

its too slow, wish it was faster paced.i also feel like the court should be bigger, as sometimes it feels like it is impossible to catch the disc. kind of fun for a little bit but i found myself wishing it was faster paced. kind of cool though!! i reccomend trying it! but, youll probably wish it was faster paced too

Not windjammers.

While some of the changes like implementing duos, having a different shielding mechanic and everything are welcome, it doesn't translate the spirit of windjammers. I've played windjammers at lightning fast speed over kaillera netplay with 50ping. This game just doesn't translate the concept well due to the camera being terribly placed, to the point it breaks the concept of velocity and tracks the player too much, causing a load of confusion.

Nice attempt, nice execution on the graphics department (don't like the character design though), but bad execution of the concept.

Scratches the same itch as Rocket League with the speed and explosive rapid fire plays like Lethal League. As a fan of the original Windjammers, I was really hyped to (casually) discover this game. A fast paced tennis-like with discs and skill moves, that can be picked up almost instantly but has lots of depth, is a good formula for a competitive game. Generally, it does plenty of things right and is quite entertaining. However, it's far from perfect.

First things I noticed immediately are the camera angle and the fast animations. The low camera angle is intuitive in making shots, but awkward to project incoming disc trajectories. Probably this is something I may get used to, but it's still a letdown. The animations on the other hand, are too fast, with too few in-between frames that give a cheap feeling.

The other major issue is matchmaking. I get it there are too few players, but at least make the experience a decent one. I have to manually start matchmaking at least 3-4 times to find a match. If one match drops, it should automatically restart and search for a new one.

Finally, the number of players. It looks like the game is slowly dying and that's a pity and loss of potential. I think a free-to-play model and some aggressive push into the competitive market would help, although maybe it's too late. Worth a try though.
after each and every game the lobby and need to reconnect to server is very annoying. Bt fun game.

It's fun but just made me want to play Wind Jammers on my NeoGeo so I did.  I suggest using a arcade stick if you have one. Different sized arenas with bounce obstacles would be great, again, like Wind Jammers.
 
 The camera needs to be raised and at more of a 45-60 degree angle.

Ok, I see a lot of mixed reviews. I really have to say I appreciate this game to the fullets because as of TODAY, i was JUST playing WINDJAMMERS in a retro arcade bar and I had no idea this game existed!

It does very well with mimicking the games mechanics and feel. It has the same clunky controls, lack of physics (aside from ragdoll) and fun special charged throws! What's amazing is that there are differnet characters that look and feel a bit different. 

The original Windjammers had a Strength and Speed ratio. It felt similar here.

Though only spending a bit of time in the game for free weekend, I have to say I can find myself loving this game but also fear the online games would have thin mortality and would be left with only friends to play with (which is cool, but it's not more than a casual game I think.) Doesn't seem to get too intense.

this is a good game i guess, but.. rocket league is a better game.

Only played a few games before work.  The gameplay is solid.  Nice competitive game with quick matches.  A full game only takes about 3 minutes.

People bitching about the item store.  You have the option to not buy shit.  Or you have the option to just save up that J currency whatever it's called and buy it yourself.  I have like 15 minutes played and almost already have an item.  None of it is required.  Doesn't effect gameplay at all.

Is the game fun?  Then stfu.  You don't even need cosmetics.  You can barely even see them.  The game is fun because of the skill curve and fast and fun gameplay.  Not because of fucking hats.  Just don't buy them.
